{{Image Banner|image=Screenshot 2025-02-25 110950-elevators.png|caption=
Just when you thought your demise was inevitable, you find it. An elevator to safety.
}}
----
''' Description '''
----
{{Blurimage|file=Screenshot 2025-02-25 110950-elevators.png|caption=Two elevator shafts in an unknown level.|hovertext=Take a ride up.}} '''Object 34''', or '''Elevators''', are one of the most sought after and useful objects throughout the Backrooms. Instances of '''Object 34''' manifest as traditional office elevators, though instead of traveling between floors of a building, '''Elevators''' travel between levels. Upon entering an elevator, one will notice a series of four to fifty buttons, each marked with a number. Each instance of '''Object 34''' only serves a finite number of levels which is labeled by the buttons. Once one pushes a button for their desired level, the doors of the '''Elevator''' will close, before it ascends or descends to the requested level depending on one's position in the Backrooms at the current moment. The appearance of an '''Elevator''' remains starkly similar no matter which level it is located in. Each '''Elevator''' possesses metallic gray walls, tiled flooring, and overhead ceiling lighting. Though variations on appearance exist, they are mostly confined to the rooftop lighting, floor design, and the position of the buttons. '''Elevator''' shafts also retain the same appearance, though their placement varies widely. While '''Elevator''' shafts are usually found in typical locations such as interior hallways, '''Elevators''' can be found in more unorthodox places, such as within trees or hills, behind doors or windows, and much more. Most '''Elevators''' travel along fixed routes and remain in the same place. As a result, many colonies and outposts are centered around '''Elevators''', using them for all means such as exploring and documenting new levels and collecting resources. Within many '''Elevators''', any colonies and outposts that are accessible from the '''Elevator''' are marked via various means such as tape and marker over the buttons. The quantity of '''Elevators''' on levels decreases as the number grows higher. For instance, [[Level 0]] possesses the most '''Elevators''', while [[Level 997]] only contains two elevators. Anomalous levels are completely inaccessible via traditional '''Elevators''' and can only be accessed using '''Random-Chance Elevators''' which is a risky move. This, strangely, includes [[Level 999]], which contains no '''Elevators''' whatsoever and is treated similar to an anomalous level within the '''Elevator''' system. Currently, the longest '''Elevator''' route is between [[Level 0]] and [[Level 989]], avoiding the nine penultimate levels completely.
----
''' Variations '''
----
----
''' Random Chance Elevators '''
----
'''Random Chance Elevators''' are an unstable variant of '''Elevators''' that can be used to access every known level in the Backrooms, though comes at a serious price. Within '''Random Chance Elevators''', the buttons within regular '''Elevators''' are replaced by a singular button at the center. '''Random Chance Elevators''' takes one to a completely random level of the Backrooms with each level having an equal chance. Unlike regular '''Elevators''', '''Random Chance Elevators''' can be found in random and often anomalous places, such as atop buildings, clipped into walls or other objects, and in deep underground/underwater locations. Upon the arrival of a '''Random Chance Elevator''' on any level, obstacles are moved to different positions, and a shaft is formed that resembles conventional '''Elevator''' shafts. Once a '''Random Chance Elevator''' leaves, the shaft will vanish, and the area reverts to its original state. Due to the instability of '''Random Chance Elevators''' and risk of being sent to a dangerous level, it is advised for one to avoid using '''Random Chance Elevators''' unless it is absolutely necessary.
----
''' Keypad Elevators '''
----
'''Keypad Elevators''' are similar to typical '''Elevators''', though the button panel is replaced by a keypad with a typical QWERTY keyboard format. Similar to '''Random Chance Elevators''', '''Keypad Elevators''' are believed to be able to access any level. However, each level is connected to a code that one must enter to travel to their desired level. Many available codes within '''Keypad Elevators''' are still unknown, though a few have been mapped out as of now: *"0" sends one to [[Level 0]] *"FR33 P@RK!NG" sends one to [[Level 1]]. *"H0TEL" sends one to [[Level 5]]. *"B00!" sends one to [[Level 9.31]]. *"WALSPT" (We All Love Some Pool Time) sends one to [[Level 37]]. *"G3N3RATING" sends one to [[Level 66]]. *"THE 0TH3R H0TEL" sends one to [[Level 187]]. *"THE 0TH3R 0TH3R H0TEL" sends one to [[Level 188]]. *"26032009" sends one to [[Absolute Infinity Of Unknown]]. *"1000" sends one to [[Level 398]]. *"N30N!" sends one to [[Level 399]]. *"THE W@TERP@RK" sends one to [[Level 969]]. *"THE TOWER" sends one to [[Level 989]]. *"TICK3TS PL3@SE" sends one to [[Level 36]]. Many other codes probably exist, though they remain undiscovered. It is unknown whether levels that have unique, specific methods of entry can be accessed through '''Keypad Elevators'''. Due to the semi-unpredictable nature of '''Keypad Elevators''', attempting to discover new codes is risky. Unless one is thoroughly prepared for new levels, it is advised to only use the codes listed above.
----
''' Hazard: Malfunctions '''
----
{{Collapse|num=1|initial='''>Open Attached File'''|final='''>Close Attached File'''|body=
M.E.G. Base Alpha
The following log was documented between a M.E.G. interviewer and a wanderer going by the pseudonym "B.J", who encountered a formerly unknown hazardous property of '''Object 34'''. The wanderer was injured but made a full recovery.
''
'' ——
'''Interviewer:''' Hello, you must be the one who the two found and brought here, is that correct? '''B.J:''' Yes. '''Interviewer:''' If you are comfortable with it, please state your name. '''B.J:''' I prefer to go by B.J. '''Interviewer:''' Alright B.J, you said earlier that you got injured as a result of an '''Elevator'''. We have never heard of cases like yours before. So, is it okay if you elaborate on that? '''B.J:''' Alright, so I was on [[Level 3|3]] after an accidental noclip from [[Level 1|1]] brought me there. I was trying to survive and potentially find an exit to [[Level 4|4]] or any safer level. Suddenly, [[Entity 4|one of those moth things]] appeared out of nowhere and started chasing me. I ran as fast as I could, but the thing was on my heels. '''B.J:''' Suddenly, I see an '''Elevator''' off in the distance. I ran full speed to it and pressed the button. The thing opened, and I quickly pressed the first button I saw. The moth almost flew into the '''Elevator''', but the doors closed in time, and I crushed the thing. '''B.J:''' The '''Elevator''' went up, and I was just relieved that I escaped the moth within seconds of it mutilating me. But my relief didn't last long, as suddenly the whole elevator went black and stopped midway. It was so dark that I couldn't even see my fingers. Then, the whole thing just started falling, faster and faster. I remembered the procedure they taught in school, to lay down on the floor and cover your head. So I did it, out of pure instinct. I prayed and prayed to whatever god heard me for me to survive. '''B.J:''' Immediately after I said the prayer, the elevator suddenly stopped, as if it hit some concrete floor. Whatever happened after that is a blur. The only thing I remember is the two guys finding me and bringing me here. The prayer must have worked, as I would have probably died in there if they didn't find me. '''Interviewer:''' I see. Thank you for your time. ——
}}
'''Malfunctions''' are deadly properties specific to '''Object 34''' manifesting as events occurring as a result of anomalous electrical/structural failure. The mechanism behind '''Malfunctions''' are currently unknown, with the only known information being that '''Malfunctions''' happen once every 1000 rides using '''Elevators''', or a 0.1% chance. The following is a list of documented '''Malfunction''' events from survivors or witnesses.
Report 1:
'''Date:''' 1/5/16 ---- Summary: Two wanderers enter an '''Elevator''' located on [[Level 19]]. After a few minutes of riding, the '''Elevator''' catches on fire. Luckily, it happened to stop near [[Level 238]], and one wanderer was able to leave via a crowbar at his disposal. Once the door is pried open, the other wanderer managed to escape. Both wanderers survived, albeit with serious injuries. ---- '''Cause:''' Most likely a spark resulting from an incorrectly placed wire. ---- '''Notes:''' First encounter with a '''Malfunction''' overall.
Report 2:
'''Date:''' 11/14/18 ---- Summary: A group of five wanderers enter an '''Elevator''' on [[Level 20]]. 5 minutes into the ride, the '''Elevator''' suddenly falls down and hits another '''Elevator''' traveling on the same route containing three wanderers. All three wanderers on the bottom '''Elevator''' perish on impact, along with two from the top '''Elevator'''. The three remaining wanderers use brute force to burst open the doors, revealing them floating within [[The Void]], though right on top of [[Level 36]]. After a coordinated leap of faith, two wanderers manage to land on Level 36, while one misses. The remaining two wanderers miraculously only had minor injuries, while the last one has not been recovered. ---- '''Cause:''' Overcapacity ---- '''Notes:''' None.
Report 3:
'''Date:''' 7/24/20 ---- Summary: After B.J's interview, a security camera was placed within a few elevators serving [[Level 399]] and [[Level 499]]. One of those cameras captured an exploration team from the 399 Megacolony boarding the '''Elevator''' and pressing the button for [[Level 205]]. 35 minutes into the ride, a fire starts in the southeast corner, igniting an oxygen tank the team was carrying. Immediately, the tank explodes, bursting the '''Elevator''' and its occupants into flames. All six members of the team died due to the incident. ---- '''Cause:''' Same as Report 1. ---- '''Notes:''' This began the start of a measure to include security cameras within '''Elevators''' to track the frequency of '''Malfunction''' events.
Report 4:
'''Date:''' 9/7/21 ---- Summary: After the above report, more security cameras were installed on '''Elevators'''. Another camera captured a wanderer boarding an '''Elevator''' from [[Level 622]], bound for [[Level 324]]. An hour into the ride, the entire '''Elevator''' becomes shrouded in darkness and comes to a halt. As the camera's night vision functionality turns on, it captures the wanderer using a flashlight to find something to break down the '''Elevator's''' doors with. Upon finding none, the wanderer proceeds to using his body weight to break the doors down. After a few tries, he succeeds, and he rolls onto the grounds of [[Level 500]]. ---- '''Cause:''' Faulty lighting. ---- '''Notes:''' This is currently the least deadly '''Malfunction''' event documented.
Even though '''Malfunction''' events are highly unlikely, it is advised for one to watch for any flaws or errors if one utilizes an '''Elevator'''. Since '''Malfunctions''' are more likely to happen within '''Random Chance Elevators''', this further encourages the use of those types of '''Elevators''' only when absolutely necessary.
----
''' Do's and Dont's '''
----
----
''' Do's '''
----
*Do a thorough investigation of each '''Elevator''' before using one, unless it is an urgent situation *Come prepared for anything if you choose to take an '''Elevator'''. *Mark any viable outposts or safe resources above the buttons.
----
''' Dont's '''
----
*Assume nothing will happen. *Attempt to tamper with any feature of the '''Elevator''' (button mashing also counts). *Use '''Random Chance Elevators''' frequently.
